module.name: sort-functions
module.description: Require top-level functions to be sorted alphabetically.

scenarios.name: should allow top-level functions declared in alphabetical order
scenarios.given: a module with function alpha() before function beta() before function gamma()

scenarios.name: should allow a single top-level function
scenarios.given: a module containing only one function declaration

scenarios.name: should report functions declared out of alphabetical order
scenarios.given: a module where function zebra() is declared before function apple()
scenarios.then: a sortFunctions violation is reported on the out-of-order function

scenarios.name: should autofix a full sortable function run in one pass
scenarios.given: a module with several top-level functions in reverse alphabetical order
scenarios.then: one sortFunctions violation is reported and the autofix replaces the sortable function span with all functions in alphabetical order

scenarios.name: should move owned comments with a sorted declaration block
scenarios.given: two out-of-order sortable functions where each declaration has one or more directly attached leading comments and optional same-line trailing comments
scenarios.then: a sortFunctions violation is reported and the autofix moves the owned comments together with the reordered declaration block

scenarios.name: should report without autofixing when directive or ambiguous interstitial comments are present
scenarios.given: two out-of-order sortable functions separated by directive comments or standalone comments between declaration blocks
scenarios.then: a sortFunctions violation is reported but no autofix is offered because comment ownership is unsafe
